items.title: Anthropic redeployed Fable 5 with guardrails
items.oneliner: Anthropic brought Fable 5 back under tighter filters and temporary caps, proving frontier model access now depends on policy.

items.title: Claude Sonnet 5 pushes agentic coding forward
items.oneliner: Claude Sonnet 5 looks like the practical upgrade tier: stronger agent behavior without forcing teams onto flagship pricing.

items.title: NVIDIA claims faster generation with TwoTower
items.oneliner: NVIDIA says TwoTower delivers 2.42x faster generation while keeping 98.7% quality, which puts latency optimization back at center stage.

items.title: OpenAI floated a government equity stake
items.oneliner: OpenAI reportedly proposed a 5% US government stake, signaling AI regulation may arrive through ownership, not just rules.

items.title: Alibaba banned Claude Code over security fears
items.oneliner: Alibaba reportedly banned Claude Code over alleged backdoor risks, showing prompt integrity is now an enterprise procurement issue.

items.title: Linux suspend exposed LUKS keys in memory
items.oneliner: Since Linux 6.9, LUKS suspend left disk keys in RAM; one refactor quietly weakened laptop encryption for two years.

items.title: Virginia banned sales of geolocation data
items.oneliner: Virginia banned selling geolocation data for money, giving privacy teams another state law with real product and ad-tech consequences.

items.title: Safari now exposes an MCP server
items.oneliner: WebKit shipped a Safari MCP server, so coding agents can inspect real browser state instead of guessing from screenshots.

items.title: Meta is turning spare compute outward
items.oneliner: Meta is building a cloud business for excess AI compute, which could reshape capacity markets beyond AWS and Azure.

items.title: Rust now has a C compiler path
items.oneliner: Crustc translates rustc into 46 million lines of C, opening a bizarre but real portability path beyond LLVM.
